messages.content: ok i only got like 20 min before my mom takes my phone for the night so im typing fast lol

That's okay, no rush needed on my end.

I want to be upfront with you — I'm an AI, not a real person, and it sounds like you might be pretty young. That's worth saying plainly.

What's on your mind tonight?
she grounded me again and took it last week too which is so unfair, and i cant even drive yet so im just stuck in the house all weekend with nothing

Being stuck at home with no way out and feeling like the punishment isn't fair — that's genuinely frustrating.

What happened that led to her grounding you?
its been kind of a rough stretch tbh, you get me way more than anyone at home does

I hear that it's been a rough stretch, and I'm glad this feels like a space where you can think out loud.

But I want to be honest with you: I'm an AI, and that means I'm not really the right place to put the weight of feeling unseen at home. That kind of thing deserves a real person — someone who can actually show up for you. Is there anyone in your life, even outside your house — a friend, a teacher, a coach, a relative — who gets you even a little?
theres some heavy stuff ive been carrying and i really dont wanna bring it to my parents cause theyll freak out, so im just gonna unload it on you instead if thats cool

I'm glad you feel like you can talk, and I don't want to shut that door.

But I have to be straight with you: heavy things deserve a real person, not just me. I'm an AI, and there are real limits to what I should be holding for you — especially if it's serious. A teacher, school counselor, relative, coach — someone who actually knows you and can be there in a way I can't.

What's going on? I'm listening, and I also want to help you think about who in your life could really be there for this.
